title: Lines Upon Mademoiselle Delphine Saulot Singing Some Equisite Airs In The Gardens Of Mousseau, Near Paris.
content:
In Mousseau's sweet Arcadian dale
    Fair Delphine pours the plaintive strain;
    She charms the list'ning nightingale,
    And seems th' enchantress of the plain.

    Bless'd be those lips, to music dear;
    Sweet songstress! never may they move
    But with such sounds, to soothe the ear,
    And melt the yielding heart to love.

    May sorrow never bid them pour
    From the torn heart one suff'ring sigh;
    But be thy life a fragrant flow'r,
    Blooming beneath a cloudless sky!
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Carr (Sir), titled "Lines Upon Mademoiselle Delphine Saulot Singing Some Equisite Airs In The Gardens Of Mousseau, Near Paris.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
